questions.question: Which war was fought between the North and South in the United States?
questions.explanation: The American Civil War (1861–1865) was fought between the Union (Northern states) and the Confederacy (Southern states). The primary cause was slavery. The Union won, and the 13th Amendment abolished slavery. It remains the deadliest war in American history with an estimated 620,000 deaths.
questions.memory_hook: Civil War: brother vs. brother, North vs. South, 1861–1865.

questions.question: What was the name of the first moon landing mission?
questions.explanation: Apollo 11 was the first crewed mission to land on the Moon, on July 20, 1969. Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walked on the surface while Michael Collins orbited above. Armstrong's famous words: 'That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.'
questions.memory_hook: Apollo 11: the 11th try was the first to land. One giant leap.

questions.question: What color is the first place jersey in the Tour de France?
questions.explanation: The leader of the Tour de France wears the yellow jersey (maillot jaune). It was first awarded in 1919 and is the most prestigious prize in cycling. The green jersey goes to the best sprinter, the polka-dot jersey to the best climber, and the white jersey to the best young rider.
questions.memory_hook: Yellow jersey = Tour leader. Like a yellow highlighter marking the best.

questions.question: Which city has hosted the most Summer Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: London has hosted the Summer Olympic Games three times: 1908, 1948, and 2012. It is the only city to have hosted three Summer Olympics. Paris has hosted twice (1900, 1924) and is set to host again in 2024. Los Angeles hosted in 1932 and 1984.
questions.memory_hook: London: three-time Summer Olympics host. The city keeps coming back.

questions.question: In swimming, what stroke is swum in the first leg of an individual medley?
questions.explanation: In the individual medley, swimmers complete each stroke in this order: butterfly, backstroke, breaststroke, freestyle. In relay medleys, the order is: backstroke, breaststroke, butterfly, freestyle. The butterfly stroke is generally considered the most physically demanding.
questions.memory_hook: Butterfly FIRST in IM — fly before you float on your back.

questions.question: Which band sang 'Sweet Home Alabama'?
questions.explanation: 'Sweet Home Alabama' was released by Lynyrd Skynyrd in 1974. It was written partly as a response to Neil Young's songs 'Southern Man' and 'Alabama,' which criticized the South. The song has become one of the most recognizable rock anthems of all time.
questions.memory_hook: 'Sweet Home Alabama' — Lynyrd Skynyrd said it with a Southern drawl.

questions.question: Which actress played Katniss Everdeen in 'The Hunger Games' films?
questions.explanation: Jennifer Lawrence played Katniss Everdeen in all four Hunger Games films (2012–2015). She was 21 when the first film was released and had already won an Academy Award for Best Actress for Silver Linings Playbook. The franchise grossed nearly $3 billion worldwide.
questions.memory_hook: Lawrence as Katniss — she's on fire. Literally, her character is called 'the girl on fire.'

questions.question: Who wrote and performed 'Imagine'?
questions.explanation: 'Imagine' was written and recorded by John Lennon and released in 1971. It is widely regarded as one of the greatest songs ever written, promoting peace, harmony, and a world without divisions. Lennon called it 'virtually the Communist Manifesto' but wrapped in sugar.
questions.memory_hook: 'Imagine' — Lennon asked us to picture a better world. Simple piano, profound words.

questions.question: The 'Boston Tea Party' was a protest against taxation by which country?
questions.explanation: The Boston Tea Party occurred on December 16, 1773, when American colonists disguised as Mohawk Indians boarded British ships and dumped 342 chests of tea into Boston Harbor. It was a protest against the Tea Act and British taxation without colonial representation.
questions.memory_hook: Boston Tea Party: 'No taxation without representation' — they threw the tea out.

questions.question: Which scientist developed the theory of general relativity?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein published his general theory of relativity in 1915, describing gravity as a curvature of spacetime caused by mass and energy. The theory predicted phenomena including gravitational waves, black holes, and the bending of light around massive objects — all later confirmed.
questions.memory_hook: Einstein bent space with his mind — general relativity is his masterpiece.

questions.question: Which desert is the largest hot desert in the world?
questions.explanation: The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert, covering about 9.2 million square kilometers across northern Africa — roughly the size of the contiguous United States. It spans 11 countries. Despite its reputation, only about 25% of the Sahara is sand dunes; most of it is rocky plateau.
questions.memory_hook: Sahara = largest hot desert. (Antarctica is technically the largest desert overall — cold deserts count too.) The Sahara is as big as the US and most of it is rock, not sand.

questions.question: What type of rock is formed when lava cools and solidifies?
questions.explanation: Igneous rocks form when magma (underground) or lava (above ground) cools and crystallizes. 'Igneous' comes from the Latin for 'fire.' Granite is a common intrusive igneous rock (cooled underground); basalt is a common extrusive one (cooled above ground). About 95% of Earth's crust is igneous rock.
questions.memory_hook: Igneous = fire rock. Lava cools, igneous forms. The other two types are sedimentary (layers of sediment) and metamorphic (rock transformed by heat and pressure).
